request.question: What was the second album by the singer who was a client of Andros Rodriguez, and whose debut album was  "Santogold"?

title: Santigold discography
The Explorers Club (band)
EggHunt Records
Andros Rodriguez
Against All Will
Santigold
Noah Georgeson
The Shortwave Set
Sarah Perrotta
Richard Davies (musician)
sentences: Santi White, better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Sant"o"gold, a stage name under which most of her material has been released), is an American songwriter, producer, and singer.
White's discography under the name Santogold consists of her self-titled debut album, "Santogold", and four promotional singles, a mixtape, and four music videos.
All of these were released in 2008, except the single "Creator", which was released in late 2007.
"Santogold" peaked at number 74 on the "Billboard" 200, and reached numbers 2 and 6 on "Billboard"' s Dance/Electronic Albums and Independent Albums, respectively.
The album also charted in Belgium, France, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.
The Explorers Club are a Pop rock band originally from the coast of South Carolina.
They are a 5-man band whose debut album was released by Dead Oceans.
The music of their first album is heavily influenced by the vocal harmony styles and production of The Beach Boys.
They are also influenced by classic rock and roll arrangements as made popular by The Beatles, Chuck Berry, Phil Spector, The Zombies, The Monkees and The Byrds.
Their second album shows more influences of soft-pop artists of the early-1970s such as Burt Bacharach, Glen Campbell, etc.
The band relocated to Nashville, TN in 2014 and released their third studio album Together on June 24, 2016
EggHunt Records is an independent record label based in Richmond, Virginia.
The label was founded by Adam Henceroth and Gregory Gendron in spring of 2014.
EggHunt Records focuses on upcoming, independent artists and releases material on cassette, CD, and vinyl physical formats, as well as digital download and streaming online.
EggHunt Records maintains an active roster of artists who have toured with notable acts such as The War on Drugs and Houndmouth.
EggHunt Records alumni include Lucy Dacus, whose debut album, "No Burden", was released on EggHunt Records in 2016.
In 2017, EggHunt Records signed Eric Slick of Dr. Dog to release his solo debut album, "Palisades".
Andros Rodriguez is a multi-platinum, Grammy award-winning music producer, engineer and mixer.

His diverse catalog of clients include Pharrell, Justin Timberlake, Christina Aguilera, Florence + the Machine, Shakira, Bleachers, Whitney Houston, Cobra Starship, Santigold, Jewel, Lenny Kravitz, Kelly Clarkson, Girls' Generation, Patti Labelle, James Blunt, Ludacris, and Huang Zitao.

Against All Will is a rock quartet from Los Angeles, California, whose debut album lineup was formed in Spring 2009 by Jimmy Allen (formerly of Puddle of Mudd), Jeff Current (formerly of Seven Story Drop), Steve "Boomstick" Wilson (formerly of Dead Kennedys and t.A.T.u.), and Cello Dias (formerly of Soulfly).
Songs "All About You" and "The Drug I Need" from their debut album entitled A Rhyme & Reason ranked in the national rock radio top 50 in 2010.
Santi White (born September 25, 1976), better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Santogold), is an American singer and producer.
Her debut album, "Santogold", including the singles "Creator" and "L.E.S. Artistes", received very favorable reviews in 2008.
Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012.
In February 2016, she released her third album "99¢".
Noah Georgeson is a Grammy winning musician, producer, and solo recording artist.
Georgeson's debut album "Find Shelter" was released through Plain Recordings on November 28, 2006.
Born in San Anselmo, California, he moved with his family to Nevada City, California at the age of three.

Georgeson studied classical guitar and music composition, receiving his BA in composition from San Francisco State University in 2001, and, with a recommendation from Terry Riley, he attended Mills College, receiving his Master of Fine Arts in 2003.
While at Mills, Georgeson studied with Fred Frith, Pauline Oliveros, and Alvin Curran.
Georgeson first found popular success as a part of San Francisco band The Pleased, along with fellow member Joanna Newsom, whose debut album "The Milk-Eyed Mender" he produced.

As a musician, producer, and mixer, Georgeson has since worked with Devendra Banhart, The Strokes, Little Joy, Bert Jansch, Charlotte Gainsbourg, Robin Pecknold, Mason Jennings, Cedric Bixler, Adam Green, Os Mutantes, Adan Jodorowsky, Harper Simon, Flo Morrissey, Cate Le Bon, and Rodrigo Amarante.
The Shortwave Set are a British alternative pop band whose debut album, "The Debt Collection", was released in 2005 on Independiente Records.
The band's music combines sample-based music with more traditional songwriting and instrumentation to create a sound the group have described as "Victorian Funk".
The band's second album, "Replica Sun Machine", was released on 12 May 2008 by Wall of Sound.
Sarah Perrotta (born 1979, United States) is a Hudson Valley based singer, songwriter and pianist.
Perrotta was the front woman of the indie-rock band Outloud Dreamer whose debut album “Drink The Sky” was named best modern rock album of 2000 by WKZE Radio 98.1FM.
Perrotta’s second album “The Well” was self-released in 2008 and features Tony Levin on bass and Garth Hudson on accordion.
The album was mixed by Grammy Award winner Malcolm Burn.
Her most recent record is entitled tonight released under her surname.
Richard Davies is an Australian-American musician.
He first came to prominence in the early 1990s as leader of the Australian band The Moles.
Upon moving to the United States, Davies joined with Eric Matthews to form Cardinal, whose debut album, "Cardinal," was released by Flydaddy in 1994.
He has since released several solo albums including "There's Never Been a Crowd Like This" (1996), "Telegraph" (1998), and "Barbarians" (2000).
In 2009 Davies and Guided by Voices front man Robert Pollard, collaborating under the name Cosmos, released an album, "Jar of Jam / Ton of Bricks."
In 2012 Davies and Matthews reformed Cardinal and released a second album, "Hymns."

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Produce a compact, structured first-hop summary that helps the next agent fetch the missing second-hop facts. The summary must be one labeled block with these four short sections (each 1–4 lines):

1) Evidence found — For each concrete target term or name mentioned in the passages, list the target and which passage(s) mention it, and quote a short verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or any exact numeric/date token from the passage. Preserve the quoted text and any numeric/date formatting exactly as it appears in the passages. Include passage identifier(s) exactly as given (title or index).

2) Ambiguities — Enumerate any names or terms that correspond to multiple candidate pages or where the passages return multiple variants or conflicting values. For each ambiguous item, list the candidate labels or note the conflict using only tokens from the passages where possible.

3) Bridge / missing fact — State exactly which relation(s) or fact(s) needed to answer the question are not present or are unresolved in the provided passages (e.g., birth date, publication year, author of adaptation, founding year, award count). Be explicit and concise.

4) Retrieval direction — Provide 2–4 concrete next-hop retrievals: specific page titles or disambiguation pages, or precise search queries the next agent should run to fetch the missing bridge facts. Use actionable, precise phrasing (page title or quoted query). End this section with an explicit deferral of the final answer when the passages do not unambiguously supply all required facts.

Formatting and behavior rules:
- Produce only the single field `summary` as the output. The `summary` text must follow the four labeled sections above.
- Always quote verbatim evidence (names, short excerpts, dates, numbers) exactly as in the passages; do not reformat numeric/date tokens.
- Do NOT produce a final answer here unless every required fact to resolve the question is present and unambiguous in the provided passages (i.e., each named entity corresponds to a single candidate and the needed relation value is explicitly present). If facts are unambiguous and complete, you may state that and include a concise final conclusion in the `summary`; otherwise explicitly defer (for example: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.").
- If the natural summary would otherwise be a single bare token or name, append a one-line bridge clause that identifies the missing relation and at least one retrieval target rather than ending with the bare token.
- Keep phrasing concise and factual; favor short lines, source-linked evidence excerpts, and explicit next-step instructions. Do not attempt to fetch pages yourself—only list retrieval targets/queries.

Example structure to follow in the `summary` field (use passage text and titles exactly):
Evidence found: ...
Ambiguities: ...
Bridge / missing fact: ...
Retrieval direction: ...

End of instructions.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include both entity tokens from the inputs and a single neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the evidence bridge to retrieve (e.g., "EntityA EntityB director").
- If the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single text field only):
- Output exactly one concise summary string (no extra fields, labels, or metadata). Keep the summary to 1–2 short sentences unless the evidence forces a single-token identification (see below).
- Primary goal: integrate the first-hop identity in `context` with facts found in `passages` so the final answer agent has the explicit, supported linkage it needs.

When the question requires linking the context entity to information in the passages and the passages supply the bridging fact:
- Produce a concise (1–2 sentence) integrated summary that (1) names the context entity (using the form present in `context`), (2) names the supporting entity/attribute found in `passages` (using the form present in the inputs), and (3) states the explicit relation or fact (membership, creator attribution, partnership, founding/establishment, award, etc.) exactly as supported by the inputs. Use only facts present in `context` and `passages` and avoid introducing any unsupported details.

Name and attribution formatting:
- If a canonical multi-token name (given + family name or full official name) appears in the inputs, use that full form in the summary; do not truncate to a surname or partial name.
- For questions about creator/author/credit, prefer the person-name attribution as given in the inputs over returning the title of a work or event.

Identification requests:
- If the question explicitly asks for a single identification (for example, asks "which X" or otherwise requests a named token or label), return exactly the canonical name(s) as presented in the passages. If multiple valid names are requested, return them comma-separated. Do not add explanatory clauses, relational fragments, or extra text in these cases—only the canonical name(s).

When evidence is missing or ambiguous:
- If the passages do not supply a clear bridging fact, state that succinctly (e.g., "No supporting evidence in the provided passages" or "Unclear from the provided sources"). Retain uncertainty rather than asserting unsupported facts.

Conciseness and fidelity constraints:
- Keep the summary short and factual (1–2 sentences). Do not expand into long biographies, exhaustive lists, or unrelated details.
- Do not assert facts that are not present in `context` and `passages`.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passage text, but short verbatim names or phrases necessary for fidelity are allowed.
- Treat the `context` field as the primary first-hop identity to be linked to `passages` when integrating evidence.

Produce the `summary` now based only on the supplied `question`, `context`, and `passages`.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Use summary_1 and summary_2 as the sole evidential basis for the final answer; give greatest weight to summary_2 (the later, post-retrieval integrated evidence). Do not introduce facts from outside these summaries.

Output rules (apply in order):

1) If summary_1 and summary_2 clearly and unambiguously agree on the same concise answer, set the value of `answer` to exactly one minimal string and nothing else:
   - For a binary yes/no conclusion, emit the single lowercase token: yes or no.
   - For any other concise label, emit that label exactly as it appears in the summaries (trim leading/trailing whitespace and strip any surrounding punctuation or wrapper syntax such as surrounding parentheses, quotes, or JSON-like braces). Do not add words, sentences, punctuation, quotes, brackets, or any other surrounding text.

2) Preserve multi-token labels: if any visible summary contains a multi-token form of the answer (for example a full personal name, a multi-word company or place), output that full multi-token form verbatim. Only shorten a label if no multi-token form appears in either summary.

3) Company/developer preference: when the question requests a company or developer and the summaries show a subordinate studio together with an explicit parent/publisher label (e.g., in parentheses or with phrasing like "published by"), choose the parent/publisher name as the single minimal answer string. If no parent/publisher is explicitly indicated, choose the best-supported developer/studio name from the summaries.

4) If the summaries conflict or are ambiguous, do NOT apply the unanimous-agreement rule in (1). Instead, use summary_2 as the primary basis and emit the minimal concise string supported by summary_2, applying rules (2) and (3) as relevant. If summary_2 provides no concise supported answer, set `answer` to the minimal explicit token "unknown".

Formatting constraints (must always be followed):
- Produce exactly one field: `answer`.
- The value of `answer` must be a single minimal string (no surrounding text, punctuation, explanation, or structural wrappers).
- Do not output extra fields, labels, or markup.

Additional guidance:
- If summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge but summary_2 provides a resolved, supported conclusion, use the conclusion from summary_2.
- If a concise answer string can be drawn verbatim from the summaries, prefer that verbatim text over paraphrase.

End.

response.hop1_query: What was the second album by the singer who was a client of Andros Rodriguez, and whose debut album was  "Santogold"?
hop1_docs: Santigold | Santi White (born September 25, 1976), better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Santogold), is an American singer and producer.  Her debut album, "Santogold", including the singles "Creator" and "L.E.S. Artistes", received very favorable reviews in 2008.  Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012.  In February 2016, she released her third album "99¢".

Santogold (album) | Santogold is the debut studio album by American artist Santigold (real name Santi White, who performed under the moniker Santogold at the time of the album's release).  The album was released on April 29, 2008 in the United States and May 12 in the United Kingdom, on Downtown Records and Atlantic Records, respectively.

Santigold discography | Santi White, better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Sant"o"gold, a stage name under which most of her material has been released), is an American songwriter, producer, and singer.  White's discography under the name Santogold consists of her self-titled debut album, "Santogold", and four promotional singles, a mixtape, and four music videos.  All of these were released in 2008, except the single "Creator", which was released in late 2007.  "Santogold" peaked at number 74 on the "Billboard" 200, and reached numbers 2 and 6 on "Billboard"' s Dance/Electronic Albums and Independent Albums, respectively.  The album also charted in Belgium, France, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.

Andros Rodriguez | Andros Rodriguez is a multi-platinum, Grammy award-winning music producer, engineer and mixer.  His diverse catalog of clients include Pharrell, Justin Timberlake, Christina Aguilera, Florence + the Machine, Shakira, Bleachers, Whitney Houston, Cobra Starship, Santigold, Jewel, Lenny Kravitz, Kelly Clarkson, Girls' Generation, Patti Labelle, James Blunt, Ludacris, and Huang Zitao.

Cold Fact | Cold Fact is the debut album from American singer-songwriter Rodriguez.  It was released in the United States on the Sussex label in March 1970.  The album sold very poorly in the United States (Rodriguez was himself an unknown in the US), but managed to sell well in both South Africa and Australia, with Rodriguez touring Australia in 1979.

Red. White. Green. | Red.  White.  Green.  is the second studio album by American deathcore band Upon a Burning Body.  The album was released on March 26, 2012 through Sumerian Records.  The album was produced by Will Putney, who produced their debut album, "The World Is Ours".  All of the songs on the album are titled after films that Robert Rodriguez produced or directed, continuing a trend set from their debut album.  It is the only album with Jonathan Gonzalez on drums and the last album with Chris "CJ" Johnson on guitar.
My Versions | My Versions is the debut album of Swedish singer Robin Stjernberg who was formerly a member of the boyband What's Up!  with Eric Saade and had placed second in Idol 2011.

- Santigold — [1]: "Santigold (formerly Santogold)"
- Debut album "Santogold" — [1]: "Her debut album, "Santogold","
- Second album title — [1]: ""Master of My Make-Believe""
- Second album year — [1]: 2012
- Andros Rodriguez client list includes Santigold — [4]: "Santigold"

Ambiguities:
- Stage name variant: "Santigold" vs "Santogold" (passage shows both forms)

Bridge / missing fact:
- No missing relation: the singer who is a client of Andros Rodriguez and whose debut was "Santogold" is identified, and her second album title is explicitly present.

Retrieval direction:
- No further retrieval needed. Final answer: the second album is "Master of My Make-Believe".

Her debut album, "Santogold", including the singles "Creator" and "L.E.S. Artistes", received very favorable reviews in 2008.  Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012.  In February 2016, she released her third album "99¢".

White's discography under the name Santogold consists of her self-titled debut album, "Santogold", and four promotional singles, a mixtape, and four music videos. The album also charted in Belgium, France, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.
Lights Out (Santigold song) | "Lights Out" is the third single by American artist Santogold (now Santigold), taken from her self-titled debut "Santogold" (see 2008 in music).

L.E.S. Artistes | "L.E.S. Artistes" is the second single by American artist Santigold (known then as "Santogold"), and along with "Creator", the first to be taken from her self-titled debut "Santogold" (see 2008 in music).

Creator (song) | "Creator" is the debut single by American artist Santogold (now Santigold), and along with "L.E.S. Artistes", the first to be taken from her then self-titled debut "Santogold".  It is written by Daryn McFayden, Santi White and David Taylor.
Say Aha | "Say Aha" is the fourth single by American artist Santigold, taken from her previously self-titled debut "Santogold".
response.summary_2: Santigold's second album is "Master of My Make-Believe" (2012).
